water_temp_read.read: Water is warming; trout may favor more oxygenated water.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Chattahoochee River is fair to good with water warming to 62.6°F. Recent stocking supports a strong bite using stocked-trout attractors and egg patterns.
generated_text.best_window: The day 2 morning is best due to lower wind and clearer skies, allowing for focused nymph fishing in oxygenated runs where trout favor staying comfortable and feeding effectively.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 62.6°F with flow at 899 cfs and a gage height of 2.03 ft. Turbidity is low at 5.8 NTU, indicating clear water.
generated_text.weather_setup: Skies are partly cloudy with 19.6% average cover, pressure steady at 30.04 inHg showing a neutral trend. Wind is moderate at 5.6 mph from the west with a low chance of rain at 2%.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will position in riffles, pocket water, and runs with good oxygenation, plus seams and structure for feeding. Use clean drifts with smaller natural flies and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell nymphs early and late. Expect balanced nymph presentations, targeting active fish in the best windows without strong pressure changes.
